subject:
d'aarcy, masius, benton & bowles usa, inc. - project services
 agreement

emails.subject:
D'Aarcy, Masius, Benton & Bowles USA, Inc. - Project Services
 Agreement
emails.subject_base:
D'Aarcy, Masius, Benton & Bowles USA, Inc. - Project Services
 Agreement
emails.body: D'Aarcy, Masius, Benton Bowles USA, Inc. Project Services Agreement You forwarded to me a letter from Enron Corp. to the above vendor authorizing the use of a subcontractor by the name of Jason Yang. The letter is non-specific as to name and title but since the work is for the Online Project, I would imagine that Philippe Bibi would be the appropriate person. I have not submitted the letter to Philippe for signature for two reasons: (1) We do not have the original Agreement for Project Services. I don't know who executed it but it is not in our files. (2) There are actually a couple of standard subcontractor agreements that we ask the Contractor and Sub-Contractor to execute. I am attaching electronic copies to this e-mail and printed copies to the hard copy which I am returning to you along with the original letter. If we find the person (Corporate Officer) who signed the original Agreement for Project Services, we should have that person sign all of these documents and then formally transmit them to the vendor. We need the original agreement before we can proceed. Please call me if I can be of help. The subcontractor agreements are attached below. gene

emails.subject:
Re: D'Aarcy, Masius, Benton & Bowles USA, Inc. - Project Services
 Agreement
emails.subject_base:
D'Aarcy, Masius, Benton & Bowles USA, Inc. - Project Services
 Agreement
emails.body: Re: D'Aarcy, Masius, Benton Bowles USA, Inc. Project Services Agreement Gene, As you my recall, you returned the original subcontractor letter with respect to the referenced agreement as you were unable to locate the original Project Services Agreement in Enron's files. I was just talking to Carol St. Clair, one of the attorneys I work for, and we are of the agreement that we cannot sign this side subcontractor letter until we track down where the original agreement is. How can we locate the orignal agreement within Enron Corp.?
